soal.teks:
Teacher: 'Everybody, may I have your attention, please?'
Students: '...' (Latihan #1)
soal.pembahasan: To respond to 'asking for attention', students politely acknowledge by saying 'Yes, Ma'am' or 'Yes, Sir'.
soal.trik: Respond affirmatively when a teacher asks for attention.

soal.teks:
Andi: 'I don't think I can lift this heavy box.' 
Budi: 'Don't worry, I ... help you.' (Latihan #2)
soal.pembahasan: Budi expresses his willingness/intention to help, so 'will' is the perfect auxiliary modal to use.

soal.teks:
Siti: 'Are you sure you ... swim across this river?'
Beni: 'Yes, I am a very good swimmer.' (Latihan #3)
soal.pembahasan: The context is about 'ability' (capability) to swim. Thus, the modal 'can' is the correct one.

soal.teks: Students ... wear uniforms to school. It is an obligation. (Latihan #4)
soal.pembahasan: 'Must' indicates strong necessity or strict obligation (keharusan). Rules in school dictate students 'must' wear uniforms.

soal.teks: The sign says 'No Smoking'. It means you ... smoke here. (Latihan #5)
soal.pembahasan: 'No smoking' is a prohibition. The modal verb for strong prohibition is 'must not' or 'mustn't'.

soal.teks: If you have a toothache, you ... go to the dentist. (Latihan #6)
soal.pembahasan: 'Should' is used to give advice (saran) or recommendation. Going to the dentist is highly recommended for a toothache.

soal.teks: A text that tells a past experience or sequence of past events in order to entertain or inform is called... (Latihan #7)
soal.pembahasan: Recount text retells events or experiences in the past chronologically (e.g., 'Last holiday, I went to Bali').
soal.trik: Menceritakan masa lalu asli berurutan = Recount Text.

soal.teks: What tense is mostly used in Recount Texts? (Latihan #8)
soal.pembahasan: Because recount text tells about past events, it predominantly uses the Simple Past Tense (V2).

soal.teks:
Ari: 'Would you like to come to my dinner party tonight?' 
Lia: '... I have to finish my homework.' (Latihan #9)
soal.pembahasan: Lia provides a reason for NOT going ('have to finish homework'). So she is declining the invitation politely with 'I'd love to, but'.
soal.trik: Menolak undangan dng sopan biasanya diawali 'I'd love to, but...'

soal.teks: 'We went to the beach yesterday.' The word 'went' is the past form of the verb... (Latihan #10)
soal.pembahasan: 'Go' is verb 1 (present), 'Went' is verb 2 (past), and 'Gone' is verb 3 (past participle).
